Acid Spray Trap.name: Acid Spray Trap
Acid Spray Trap.descriptionHazard: Hidden nozzles in the ceiling spray acid pumped in from a nearby reservoir to destroy the desk and all its documents, as well as anyone trying to retrieve those documents.
Acid Spray Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable one of the two acid-spewing nozzles or @Check[crafting|dc:21] (trained) to rig a makeshift plug for one of the two nozzles
Acid Spray Trap.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute. To be reset, the trap's reserve tanks must be refilled with acid.
Acid Spray Trap.routine: (1 action) The nozzles spray acid from the ceiling onto every creature adjacent to the desk. Each affected creature takes @Damage[4d6[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save). For each broken nozzle, this damage is reduced by 2d6.

action->Ooze Surprise.name: Ooze Surprise
action->Ooze Surprise.description:
Trigger A creature flips the switches on the underside of the desk in the wrong order or a creature attempts to open one of the desk drawers without first disabling the trap

Effect A hatch opens in the ceiling, releasing a string slime onto the desk. The string slime makes a pseudopod Strike against one creature within reach before rolling initiative. The trap then rolls initiative.

action->Gummy Hands.name: Gummy Hands
action->Gummy Hands.description: Candies' residue gives Aezar an especially strong grip on her greataxe, which grants her a +2 circumstance bonus to her Reflex DC against attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} her.

action->Tooth Decay.name: Tooth Decay
action->Tooth Decay.description:
Frequent candy consumption has worsened Aezar's already poor dental health. If she takes 10 or more bludgeoning damage from a single attack or effect, she must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save.

Success Aezar yells in pain but is otherwise unaffected.

Failure Several of Aezar's teeth break. She takes a –2 penalty to damage with her jaws Strike as well as to Athletics checks made as part of her @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.Tkd8sH4pwFIPzqTr]{Grab} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.uJSseLa57HZYSMUu]{Swallow Whole} abilities. These penalties last until Aezar is restored to her Hit Point maximum.

Critical Failure As failure, but so many of Aezar's teeth break that the penalty increases to –3 and she takes @Damage[(1d6-2)[persistent,bleed]] damage.

action->Glutton's Feast.name: Glutton's Feast
action->Glutton's Feast.description:
If Aezar damages a living creature with her jaws Strike, she gains 1d6 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.LWFDNwi3iJuf476A]{Effect: Glutton's Feast (Aezar)}

action->Glutton's Rush.name: Glutton's Rush
action->Glutton's Rush.description: Aezar Strides twice and makes a jaws Strike. If she damages a living creature with this Strike, the temporary Hit Points she receives from glutton's feast is increased to 2d6.

action->Sticky Axe.name: Sticky Axe
action->Sticky Axe.description:
Trigger Aezar succeeds at a greataxe Strike against a creature

Effect Aezar's axe has a lingering residue from striking various sweets, and the sticky blade clings to her most recent victim, giving Aezar momentary leverage. Aezar can attempt to [[/act reposition]] or [[/act trip]] the target using the greataxe's reach, even though she doesn't have a free hand.

Alternatively, she can forgo either maneuver to drag the target toward her gaping mouth, granting her jaws unarmed Strike the agile trait for attacks made against the target until the end of her turn.

action->Chopping Frenzy.name: Chopping Frenzy
action->Chopping Frenzy.description:
Requirements Alendreu is wielding an orc necksplitter in each hand

Effect Alendreu lashes out with both axes. He attempts two Strikes, one with each necksplitter, each using his current multiple attack penalty. Both Strikes must have the same target, and the second strike takes a –2 penalty.

If both hit, combine their damage and apply resistances and weaknesses only once, and the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} from the fury of the blow until the start of their next turn. This counts as two attacks when calculating Alendreu's multiple attack penalty.

action->Consume Flesh.name: Consume Flesh
action->Consume Flesh.description:
Requirements The ghoul is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour.

Effect The ghoul devours a chunk of the corpse and regains @Damage[(1+(max(0,floor(@actor.level/2))))d6[healing]] Hit Points.

It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

action->Ghoul Whispers.name: Ghoul Whispers
action->Ghoul Whispers.description:
Requirements A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} creature is within the ghoul's reach

Effect The ghoul whispers dark thoughts and vile cravings into the creature's ears. The creature must save against the forbidden cravings curse.

Forbidden Cravings (curse) A creature can still eat and drink while sickened by this curse

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:21|traits:curse], using the high spell DC for the ghoul's level

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effects (1 day)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[void]] damage and the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} until it consumes raw meat (1 day)

Stage 3 as stage 2

Stage 4 as stage 2 unless the target has consumed raw meat in the past 24 hours, then it takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} until it consumes raw meat;

Stage 5 if the creature has eaten raw meat in the past 24 hours, it dies and rises as a ghoul, if not, it returns to stage 4

action->Grave Knowledge.name: Grave Knowledge
action->Grave Knowledge.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The ghoul calls upon knowledge it retains from one creature it has consumed in the past 7 days. The ghoul attempts a skill check using a skill in which the consumed creature was trained (if it's unclear whether the creature was trained, the GM decides). The ghoul is treated as trained and uses the high skill modifier for the ghoul's level ([[/gmr 1d20+12]]). This takes the same amount of actions or time as usual for the check.

The ghoul can instead automatically learn something specific known by a creature it consumed in the last 7 days, like the location of a hidden treasure or the name of a loved one. The ghoul can do this only once for a given creature, no matter how much of its flesh the ghoul consumed.

action->Construct Armor (Hardness 7).name: Construct Armor (Hardness  7)
action->Construct Armor (Hardness 7).description: Like normal objects, an animated wardrobe has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated wardrobe is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 17.

action->Clothes Grab.name: Clothes Grab
action->Clothes Grab.description: A creature hit by the animated wardrobe's clothes whip becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the animated wardrobe. The creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the reach of the wardrobe's clothes whip. A creature can sever the clothes whip with a Strike against AC 15 that deals at least 5 slashing damage. This deals no damage to the animated wardrobe.

Automatic Butler.name: Automatic Butler
Automatic Butler.publicNotes:
An automatic butler is a magical construct shaped like a wooden humanoid mannequin, usually tailored in a simple black suit and tie. It has only the barest hint of a face on its round head, though its jaw opens on a hinge to simulate regular mouth movements when it speaks. Its voice is often calm and measured, and it speaks quite politely. It follows the orders given to it by its creator (or anyone whom the creator has given permission to issue commands), which usually involves fetching items, keeping a room tidy, or ushering out guests. If unwanted guests become belligerent, an automatic butler can defend itself by battering intruders or using its innate magic to hurl objects with deadly force. Once a fight has ended, an automatic butler usually spends the next hour or so cleaning up the mess left behind. Automatic butlers are built on designs similar to soulbound dolls. Like other soulbound constructs, an automatic butler is animated by a fragment of the soul of a deceased mortal. This fragment can come from either a willing donor or an unwilling victim, who can resist the process with a successful Will save against the creator's Crafting DC. While resisting ruins the construct, the soul extraction process is often fatal, and the person dies regardless. Unlike with other soulbound constructs, the personality of the soul used to create an automatic butler is wholly suppressed. Instead, the automatic butler comports itself in the way a stereotypical servant would. Some automatic butlers are created with slightly different skill sets. They can be created to speak more languages beyond that of their creator, making them passable family diplomats who might welcome foreign guests. Some are trained to take care of tasks that require specialization, such as tutoring the youth of the household or tailoring the family's clothes. The sidebar covers some other options for a whole domestic staff of constructed servants.

Automatic Cooks and Gardeners

Should the designs for creating automatic butlers reach the wider world, someone would likely end up creating variations of the magical construct. These creatures would use the same basic statistics as an automatic butler, with a few minor changes. An automatic cook might have cleanse cuisine as an innate spell, a knife Strike, and the ability to shoot a jet of boiling hot water. An automatic gardener might have scatter scree and other spells with the earth or plant traits as innate spells, a hoe or shovel Strike, and the ability to pelt foes at range with seeds.
Automatic Butler.languagesDetails: one spoken by its creator (typically Common)

action->Soul Fragment.name: Soul Fragment
action->Soul Fragment.description: Like other soulbound constructs, an automatic butler is animated by soul energy, though the donor soul's personality is suppressed in favor of a simple demeanor, either politely helpful or stoically pragmatic. It also has none of that creature's memories. This soul fragment removes the immunity to spirit most constructs have, but because the automatic butler's personality overrides that of the original creature, the butler does have some resistance to spirit damage.

action->Right This Way.name: Right This Way
action->Right This Way.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The automatic butler attempts to usher a creature along while removing their coat. The automatic butler attempts an Athletics check to [[/act reposition]] a Large or smaller creature within reach. On a success, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical success) as the automatic butler pulls at some part of their clothing or gear. The target can remove this condition by taking an Interact action.

Balance Point.name: Balance Point
Balance Point.descriptionHazard: A 40-foot deep pit is obscured by a false floor set over a single balance point. Pressure on either side to the east or west causes occupants to fall into the depths below.
Balance Point.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to attach the balanced stone floor piece to the actual floor and lock it in place, or @Check[crafting|dc:23] (trained) to jury-rig a quick bit of extra support to prevent the floor from tilting

action->Tipping Floor.name: Tipping Floor
action->Tipping Floor.description:
Trigger A creature walks onto one side of the false floor

Effect The triggering creature falls into the pit and takes @Damage[20[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall unless they [[/act grab-an-edge dc=23]]. It's a @Check[athletics|dc:20] check to climb the pit walls.

action->Verdant Burst.name: Verdant Burst
action->Verdant Burst.description:
When a fungus leshy dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from its body, restoring @Damage[2d8[healing]] Hit Points to each fungi creature in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This area is filled with fungi, becoming difficult terrain.

If the terrain is not a viable environment for this fungi, they wither after 24 hours.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
The fungus leshy transforms into a giant mushroom or patch of fungi. This ability otherwise uses the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.dileJ0Yxqg76LMvu]{One with Plants}.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Spore Cloud.name: Spore Cloud
action->Spore Cloud.description:
A fungus leshy can unleash a cloud of spores that irritates the eyes and throats of non-fungus creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:15]. Each creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:16|options:area-effect,damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[1[persistent,poison]|options:area-damage] damage.

A creature has its vision reduced as long as the persistent damage continues and can see only within 20 feet.

action->Spores.name: Spores
action->Spores.description: A creature that takes damage from a fungus leshy's spore pod Strike must attempt a saving throw with the same DC (@Check[fortitude|dc:16]) and effect as its Spore Cloud ability

Frequency once per round

Effect The automatic butler spews a surprisingly hateful tirade at a creature it can see within 30 feet, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|options:inflicts:stupefied] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure). Bittersweet Surprise.name: Bittersweet Surprise
Bittersweet Surprise.descriptionHazard: A magic circle made of royal icing seals the trap door to the gristmill's basement shut and plays a nasty trick on anyone who tries to open it without all three of its gumdrop keys.
Bittersweet Surprise.disable: If the three magical gumdrops which act as this hazard's keys are placed at the correct points of the magic circle, its enchantment is disabled. Deciphering the correct locations requires a successful @Check[arcana|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] check; on a critical failure, the mere act of placing the gumdrops activates the Malicious Metamorphosis reaction. Once the enchantment is disabled, the magic circles can be wiped away like any regular icing.
Bittersweet Surprise.reset: If the trap door is closed without the magic circles being disturbed, the enchantment resumes as normal.
Bittersweet Surprise.stealth: @Check[arcana|dc:22] or @Check[occultism|dc:22] to unravel the circle's meaning without triggering its reaction.

action->Malicious Metamorphosis.name: Malicious Metamorphosis
action->Malicious Metamorphosis.description:
Trigger A creature touches the trap door without first placing the three enchanted gumdrop keys in their proper arrangement

Effect The magic circles prevent the trap door from opening and maliciously transform a portion of the triggering creature's body, with effects based on the result of the triggering creature's @Check[fortitude|dc:18|options:forced-movement,inflicts:clumsy,inflicts:off-guard,inflicts:prone,inflicts:slowed] save.

Critical Success The triggering creature is blown 10 feet away from the trap door and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}, but otherwise unaffected.

Success The target is blown 10 feet away from the trap door, knocked prone, and has three fingers on one of their hands transformed into gingerbread. They cannot use weapons with the two-hand or twin traits for as long as the transformation persists.

Failure As success, except the transformation also extends to the creature's entire hand and a portion of the adjoining arm. The affected creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for as long as the transformation persists.

Critical Failure As failure, except the transformation also applies to one of the triggering creature's legs. For the duration of the transformation, it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all of its speeds, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}, in addition to the effects listed above.

action->Construct Armor (Hardness 4).description: Like normal objects, an animated broom has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated broom is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks and reducing its Armor Class to 13.

action->Dust.name: Dust
action->Dust.description: A creature hit by an animated broom's bristles must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save or spend its next action coughing. Even if hit by multiple bristle attacks, the creature has to spend only 1 action coughing to clear the dust out. A creature who doesn't breathe is immune to this effect.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: Carexilia deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Swift Leap.name: Swift Leap
action->Swift Leap.description: The ghoul jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

Collapsing Ceiling.name: Collapsing Ceiling
Collapsing Ceiling.descriptionHazard: A 5-foot-square section in the ceiling is in danger of collapsing. A character can use a Crafting check instead of a Perception check to detect the hazard.
Collapsing Ceiling.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:21] (trained) to support the damaged area against a collapse

action->Falling Rocks.name: Falling Rocks
action->Falling Rocks.description:
Trigger A creature enters a square where the ceiling's structure is weakened

Effect Rocks fall in the square, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save) to anyone in that square. The square also turns into greater difficult terrain.
